mnemonics.title: Set Associativity Types
mnemonics.content: D-1, S-n, F-All: Direct=1 way, Set=n way, Fully=All ways. Remember: 'Direct Starts at 1, Sets grow progressively, Fully takes All'.

mnemonics.title: Address Decomposition
mnemonics.content: TIO - Tag Index Offset. Remember: 'Tall IeS Overpower' → Tag-Index-Offset.

mnemonics.title: Replacement Policies
mnemonics.content: LRU = Least Recently Used, FIFO = First In First Out. Remember: 'LRU Remembers Recent History, FIFO Follows Insertion Order'.

mnemonics.title: Write Policies
mnemonics.content: WT = Write Through (both), WB = Write Back (cache first). Remember: 'Through Goes Everywhere, Back Stays Put'.

acronyms.usage: Replacement policy that evicts the block not accessed for the longest time
Replacement policy that evicts the oldest block in the set
Write-back cache lines that have been modified are marked as dirty
Portion of address identifying which memory block is stored in cache line
Cache addressing scheme using virtual index and physical tag
Performance metric affected by cache hit/miss rates

visualTips.visualization: Draw address as 3 boxes: | TAG | SET INDEX | BYTE OFFSET |. Size increases left to right based on bits needed.
Think of cache as a 2D array: Rows = Sets, Columns = Ways. Each cell holds a block with tag, valid bit, dirty bit.
Flowchart: CPU request → Extract set index → Compare tags in all ways → If match and valid = HIT, else = MISS.
Direct-mapped = 1 lane road, Set-associative = multi-lane, Fully-associative = parking lot (any space). More lanes = fewer collisions.

analogies.analogy: Think of a parking lot with numbered sections (sets), each having multiple parking spaces (ways). Cars (memory blocks) can park in any space within their assigned section.
Like two friends trying to use the same locker at the gym - only one can use it at a time, causing one to lose their belongings (data). More lockers per person = fewer conflicts.
Like checking a hotel room number (set index) and then verifying the guest name (tag) to confirm it's the right room.
Like a document with unsaved changes - the dirty bit marks 'modified' so you know to save it before clearing the cache (leaving the room).
